description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a lily flower, featuring six large, white petals with subtle pink gradients at the tips and numerous dark speckles scattered across their surfaces. The petals are arranged in a radial symmetry, forming a star-like shape when viewed from above. At the center, several prominent stamens with reddish anthers and a central pistil are visible, adding to the botanical detail. The flower is attached to a long, slender green stem, which is slightly curved and supports a few elongated, lanceolate leaves. The overall structure is delicate, with the petals slightly arching outward and the leaves extending away from the stem. The coloration and fine details suggest a fresh, natural appearance typical of ornamental lilies found in urban parks or gardens.
description: A white lily flower with pink-tipped, speckled petals, a central cluster of stamens, and a slender green stem with lanceolate leaves. The petals are arranged radially, creating a star-like floral display.
description_view_0: Side view showing the arching petals, central stamens, and the curved green stem with two visible leaves.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the radial symmetry of the six white petals with pink tips and dark speckles.
description_view_2: Angled view emphasizing the petal curvature, speckling, and the extension of the stem and leaves.
description_view_3: Rear side view displaying the underside of the petals, the attachment to the stem, and the elongated leaves.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, forming a single whorl with clear actinomorphic (radial) symmetry. Each petal slightly overlaps its neighbor, creating a star-like pattern typical of many lilies.
